degraded: The external CODE review returned only ONE usable leg. Gemini's response was `feedback: null` -- an empty document, not findings. This is the THIRD consecutive code-mode degeneration (S2, S3, S4), so it is a standing provider defect rather than a one-off; recorded rather than re-rolled so the thin code-review coverage stays visible and is NOT counted as a passing review. The PLAN review had BOTH legs substantive (Gemini 5 findings, GPT 12) and every one is dispositioned in the mini-plan. GPT's code leg returned 2 findings: one false positive caused by my own exclusion of the 156 KB golden.json from the diff sent for review (verified empirically, not asserted), and one real doc/code inconsistency in convergence rule C8, fixed.

Two independent plan reviewers (Gemini LOW, GPT LOW) rated the `test_group_d_hardening.py` split as scope creep unrelated to S4. They are right in principle. It was kept because the campaign brief instructs S4 to fix it -- S3 left the file at 332 lines with no baseline entry, where CI's anti-ratchet cannot see it and Group H would report it post-merge -- and because a separate commit is not available: the F11 verifier inspects a single commit. Surfaced to the orchestrator rather than silently overridden.

The outbox sweep at worktree setup reported `protected_status_unplaceable` for trg-b1dda91d: a status line whose append is in main's tracked log but not reachable from this branch. Pre-existing and unrelated to S4; no triage append of mine was dropped. Left for the main-tree reconcile rather than worked around here.

ROUND 2 (external code review): two blocking findings, both confirmed and fixed. (1) Convergence rules C2+C3+C8 composed into a false-requirement route -- a coverage table keyed by FR id parsed as requirements, reaching DuplicateRequirementId with an unactionable message. Every guard that independently blocked it had been removed by the same change. Reproduced first, then closed with TWO mechanisms (a width bound alone fails the 3-column variant; header-governance alone fails the headerless variant). C6 WITHDRAWN and C8 RE-DECIDED rather than patched around. (2) C1's residual risk was routed to an S5 acceptance criterion that does not cover it -- verified by reading S5 -- so S5's AC was amended and two triage anchors filed in the TRACKED log. Withdrawing C6 also broke the BOM probe (a UTF-8 BOM is not whitespace to str.strip(), so a BOM'd header line never registered as a table row); fixed at the source. Blast radius of the C6 withdrawal was larger than the corpus showed: 9 hand-written test fixtures across 5 files were headerless shorthand and needed real headers. None is a producer shape -- both writers always emit a header -- but the count is worth recording.

surface_verification.note: The behavioural surface is the FR-table reader and the five consumers projecting from it. Six suites drive it: the shape contract, the TABLE BOUNDARY suite (added in round 2 -- which tables may be read as requirements at all, after code review found a composition route that admitted false ones), the boundary probes, the import-style matrix across all three ADR-045 load realms, and the two golden-corpus verdict modules pinning which false verdicts flipped and which stayed frozen.

behaviors.behavior: all five parsers agree on the ids a spec declares
the reader is a leaf -- no back-edge to any parser it replaced
all five historical table shapes parse
column order is no longer load-bearing
FV-1: a populated spec parsing to zero rows now FAILs T1
FV-3: the body column is the one the header names
FV-3: the second occurrence in the malformed fixture is fixed too
FV-4: Group I reads a table whose id column is headed FR
FV-5: every parser keeps rows after a later heading
a flipped frozen-bug entry records which run flipped it and what it reads now
absent planning tree still SKIPs
absent and empty stay indistinguishable from each other; only the populated case separated
FV-2 remains frozen (belongs to S6)
removed rows are marked, not dropped -- the half the retired drift suite never pinned
a removed section ends at the next same-or-shallower heading
C1: only the canonical two-digit id is a requirement row
C1: id validation is a full match -- near-misses are rejected
C2: the column map survives a heading and is replaced by a header
C2: a persisted column map does not capture an unrelated later table
C3: an unrecognised priority is coerced, not fatal
C4: an escaped pipe is content and does not shift columns
C4: PRODUCER->consumer round-trip through markdown_table.escape_cell
C5: indentation and a missing closing pipe do not drop a row
C6: a headerless table falls back positionally and never claims explicit layers
C7: fold-map rows are never requirements
C8: a thin row is kept with whatever it declares
an inline **REMOVED** marker does not retire the requirement (S3 premise falsified)
the reader loads and parses identically under every import style (ADR-045)
the RTM still reads every adopt/greenfield shape through its public seam
a summary/coverage table keyed by FR id yields no requirements (2- and 3-column)
C6 withdrawn: a table with no governing header yields nothing and is recorded
C8 re-decided: a row must reach its header's Priority column
closing the route did not re-introduce FV-5 (a heading alone does not end a table)
a separator row does not end a table
a non-canonical id row does not end a table (one FR-1.1 must not drop the rest)
a UTF-8 BOM on the header line does not hide the table
declined rows reach the manifest as invalid_ids with a reason
